title: The 2023 to 2025 period produced more dedicated COP action than any prior period cited in the brief.
body: This acceleration clusters around the February 2024 AU policy and stronger UNICEF and ITU engagement. Tanzania, Kenya, and Nigeria are the clearest examples of that concentration effect.

title: Francophone Africa is narrowing the policy lag through directives, ordinances, and law-level instruments.
body: Cameroon, Senegal, Mali, Djibouti, and Gabon are used to show that movement is no longer concentrated only in Anglophone jurisdictions.

title: Data protection authorities are becoming practical entry points where dedicated COP law is absent.
body: Directives from Mali and Senegal, action from Kenya’s ODPC, and South Africa’s regulator engagement on age assurance show how DPAs are becoming practical entry points.

title: Continental policy without national domestication remains the brief’s central implementation risk.
body: The Malabo Convention remains the cautionary precedent: adoption alone does not produce enforcement or durable domestic alignment.
